name: Close CRM
description: Drive Close (sales-team CRM) from any AI agent: leads, contacts, opportunities, activities (calls/emails/SMS/notes), tasks, smart views and custom fields. 16 tools, API-key Basic-auth.
instructions:
This connector uses the Close API v1 (https://developer.close.com).

**Setup**:
1. Sign in to Close → top-right avatar → **Settings → Developer → API Keys → New API Key**.
2. Name it ('AnythingMCP'), copy the key (starts with `api_`). Set `CLOSE_API_KEY`.

**Authentication**: HTTP Basic auth with username=API_KEY and password empty. The adapter passes `Authorization: Basic base64(API_KEY:)`. Same as Stripe's auth model.

**Lead is the top-level object** — a 'lead' in Close is what other CRMs call an 'account' or 'company': it groups multiple `contacts` (the actual people), `opportunities` (deal-level pipeline records), and `activities` (timeline of calls/emails/notes/SMS). When in doubt, the agent should start with `close_search_leads` (Smart View query) rather than searching contacts directly.

**Custom field IDs**: like Pipedrive, custom fields have opaque IDs. Discover via `close_list_lead_custom_fields` / `close_list_contact_custom_fields` / `close_list_opportunity_custom_fields`. Reference them in writes via `custom.cf_XYZ` keys on the resource.

**Smart Views (saved searches)**: Close's central UX is filtered queries. The API exposes them via `close_search_leads` with a `query` JSON (the same DSL the UI builds). Easiest: build the view in the UI, copy its Smart View ID, then call `close_get_smart_view` to inspect — but for ad-hoc queries the `query` param accepts the JSON directly.

**Activities are typed**: call, email, sms, note, meeting, task_completed, lead_status_change, opportunity_status_change. Use `close_create_note_activity` for plain notes; for calls/emails the SaaS expects you to use Close's built-in dialer / email integration, not POST a fake activity (the agent shouldn't fabricate a sent email).

**Pagination**: cursor-based via `_skip` + `_limit` (max 100 per page) plus `has_more` boolean. Some list endpoints return total counts in `meta`.

**Rate limits**: ~80 requests/sec burst per organization. On 429, back off — `RateLimit-Reset` header is set.

**Out of scope here**: dialer call recording, email sequences (use Close UI), reports/analytics computation, integrations (Mailchimp/Stripe sync), webhook subscription management.

tools.name: close_get_me
tools.description: Return the user the API key belongs to plus the organization context (id, name, plan, addresses). Health check + whoami.

tools.name: close_list_leads
tools.description: List leads (companies) with optional query filter and pagination. Each lead has display_name, status_id, contacts[], opportunities[], custom fields, addresses, url, description, integration_links.

query.description: Close query string — e.g. 'has:opportunities lead_status:"Customer"' or 'has:contacts'. Empty string returns all.

_fields.description: Comma-separated fields to return (sparse). Default returns all.

tools.name: close_get_lead
tools.description: Fetch one lead by ID. Returns display_name, contacts[], opportunities[], tasks[], custom fields, all the activity rollups.

tools.name: close_create_lead
tools.description: Create a new lead (company). Pass contacts inline to create them in one call. Display_name auto-derives from name if omitted.

addresses.description: Array of {address_1, address_2?, city, state, zipcode, country, label}.

contacts.description: Inline contacts to create: [{name, title?, emails:[{type,email}], phones:[{type,phone}], urls?:[{type,url}]}].

custom.description: Custom fields keyed by 'cf_XYZ' field IDs.

tools.name: close_update_lead
tools.description: Partial-update a lead. Pass only fields to change.

tools.name: close_list_contacts
tools.description: List contacts (people) — typically scoped to a lead via the lead_id query param.

tools.name: close_create_contact
tools.description: Create a contact on a lead. Required: lead_id + name. Emails/phones are arrays of {type, email|phone}.

tools.name: close_list_opportunities
tools.description: List opportunities (deals). Filter by lead_id, status_id, status_type (active/won/lost), date_won range.

tools.name: close_create_opportunity
tools.description: Create an opportunity (deal). Value is in cents (e.g. 100000 = $1000.00). value_period: one_time, monthly, annual.

tools.name: close_update_opportunity
tools.description: Update an opportunity. Common uses: set status_id (move pipeline stage), update value, set confidence.

tools.name: close_create_note_activity
tools.description: Log a free-text note on a lead (timeline). Use for AI-generated meeting notes, summaries, observations. Do NOT use for fake emails/calls (those have their own typed activities).

lead_id.description: Lead to attach the note to.

tools.name: close_list_activities
tools.description: List activities (timeline events) — call, email, sms, note, meeting, etc. Filter by lead_id, contact_id, type, date range.

_type.description: Activity type: call, email, sms, note, meeting, task_completed, etc.

tools.name: close_create_task
tools.description: Create a task on a lead. Tasks are reminders for the assignee user.

assigned_to.description: User ID to assign to (defaults to the API key's user).

tools.name: close_list_lead_statuses
tools.description: List the lead status values configured on the organization (e.g. 'Potential', 'Qualified', 'Customer', 'Bad Fit').

tools.name: close_list_opportunity_statuses
tools.description: List the opportunity (pipeline stage) status values — typically active stages + won/lost terminal states.

tools.name: close_list_lead_custom_fields
tools.description: List the custom fields defined on leads with their IDs (cf_XYZ), types, and pick-list options. Required to compose `custom` objects in create/update.
